interactions.body:
(CB CANCEL - Specific email subject lines) [RE:CreditBuilder Payment Reminder] Hi William,
 
Thanks for getting in touch.
 
I can see that you signed up for a CreditBuilder account AND applied for a $3000 loan on 10/04/2024. 

When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 1-2 years. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as payment for a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the loan term is completed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, your CreditBuilder account is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, TransformCredit can help you access funds too! At which point, any CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

You can find out more about the Transform CreditBuilder here - transformcredit.com/transform-credit-builder/ . However, I can confirm that I have canceled your CreditBuilder account, you will not be charged again. 

We still have your loan application approved for you. All you need is a cosigner, a friend or a family member who has a 750+ credit score and agrees to make the payment if you can't. Follow this link to pick up where you left off: https://tcrdt.com/s/T67NOKJ3

If you need help, please let me know
 
Have a great day, 
Jade @ Transform CreditBuilder

Transform Credit Inc.

Hi William,
Thank you for reaching out.
A suitable cosigner is a US resident, living and working in US, above the age of 23,  who has a credit score of more than 750 and a good history of paying their bills on time. They should also trust you, will pay if you do not and will give correct contact details and sign the agreement. Homeowners are much more likely to be accepted with a slightly lower score of 700 and above and a good history of paying bills on time.
Hope this helps.
Please let us know if you need any further help.
Thanks,
Transform Credit

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to litigation for misrepresentation and fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

IF THE INFORMATION BELOW IS CORRECT, YOU DO NOT NEED TO REPLY TO THIS EMAIL.

Information provided to Transform Credit by you:

Borrower: Sherita ******
IP: 174.224.112.34
DateTime (UTC): 11-15-2025
Stated Monthly Income: Part Time Employed $25000
Rent: $1200.00
Bills: $350.00
Other Expenses: $400.00
Your Credit Commitments: $0.00
Transform Credit Repayment: $167.73
To be safe: $50.00
Total available after expenditure and this loan repayment: $22832.27


Yours sincerely
Transform credit Applications Dept

[Your Transform Credit Loan Application Ref: *****************90900] Hi Glenn M,

Thanks for choosing Transform Credit, we have attached your Proposed Credit Contract with this email for you to keep. This does not mean that your application has been approved, but it gives you a chance to read and understand the terms of the agreement. 

To finalize your application please complete all of the required steps online - https://transformcredit.com/hub?aref=*****************90900 

If you have any questions about our application process please visit our website https://transformcredit.com/faq

Amber @ Transform Credit

[Affordability Confirmation] To Glenn M ******,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to charges of criminal fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

IF THE INFORMATION BELOW IS CORRECT, YOU DO NOT NEED TO REPLY TO THIS EMAIL.

Information provided to Transform Credit by you:

Borrower: Glenn M ******
IP: 73.210.87.67
DateTime (UTC): 03-20-2024
Stated Monthly Income: Full Time Employed $2700
Rent: $0
Bills: $0
Other Expenses: $440
Your Credit Commitments: $289
Transform Credit Repayment: $177.130000
To be safe: $50
Total available after expenditure and this loan repayment: $1743.870000


Yours sincerely
Transform credit Applications Dept

interactions.body: (AI BW GTQs - Secure the loan with collateral SMS) Hi Antonio, we offer unsecured loans and therefore, sadly, we can't accept car titles or home deeds as collateral. Our loans are based on trust. A cosigner shows us that someone trusts you to repay your loan, and that we should too! This trust allows us to lend to you even if you've been turned down elsewhere. If you don't have a suitable cosigner, you can reply CANCEL. Or use this link to continue when you're ready: https://tcrdt.com/s/RDR3MX9S Thanks, Transform Credit

interactions.body: (Requesting lower loan without a GT SMS) Hi Kenneth, Transform Credit requires a cosigner, regardless of the requested loan amount. A cosigner shows us that someone trusts you and that we should too, regardless of your credit score. A suitable cosigner is someone who has a very good credit history with no trouble paying bills in the past, and who can afford to make the monthly payments if you don't. Although they don't have to be a homeowner, it may increase the possibility of us accepting them as your cosigner if they do own their own home. If you need to update your loan amount or term, you can do so here: https://tcrdt.com/s/DCGKX3DG Thanks, TransformCredit

interactions.body:
[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : *****************60005] Hello Danielle, 

Thanks for reaching out. I'm sorry to hear you're having issues with the verification process.  We accept Driver’s Licenses, Passports, ID cards & residence permits issued by the US Government. You must use your physical ID, that belongs to you and is in date. Add your ID here: https://tcrdt.com/s/8E13PROL

If you are having trouble uploading your ID, make sure the photo isn’t blurry, the ID is fully visible in frame & is in a well-lit area. Also ID must be a drivers license, passport, ID card or resident permit. The website prompts you on how to correctly upload your ID. 

If you're struggling to provide camera or sound permissions to complete your ID verification, please follow:

This link for IPhone: https://tcrdt.com/s/AUA3D7JE 
This link for Android: https://tcrdt.com/s/D6B2SXSF

If you're still having difficulties after trying these solutions, please send a screenshot of the error message, including the url at the top of the screen.

Thanks, and have a great day!
LaToya@Transform Credit

Hi Danielle. Your Credit File is locked. To continue with your application, you must unlock it by contacting Experian, visiting their website or using the Experian App. Once done, reply 'UNLOCKED' to this message and we will process your application for you. Thanks, Ben at Transform Credit

interactions.body: (Borrower GT Rule 1 SMS) Hi Lucy, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, and we should too! They can be ANYONE who is a US resident, has 750+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! You'll find tips here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/1B7JOT8D - we'd love your loan to add to the +$100 million we've already paid out. If you still can't find a cosigner, please respond CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Jade, Transform Credit

(Borrower Cancellation GT Rule 1 Follow-Up SMS) Hi Lucy, we understand you aren't able to provide a cosigner, so we've canceled your application for now. If you wish to reapply in the future, please visit: https://transformcredit.com/ James, Transform Credit

interactions.body: (2FA code issues GT SMS) Hi Matthew. As a Together Loans cosigner, we send you lots of important information via SMS. It's really important that we have a cell number for you, and your borrower separately, so we can keep you both up to date with crucial information! If the code is incorrect, request a new code and enter as soon as you receive it (the code expires after a short time). If delivery of your code to your cell is taking a long time, or you're not receiving it at all, try restarting your phone to refresh network connectivity and make sure you're in an area with strong coverage. (Borrower GT Default Email) [RE:Need help finding a cosigner?] Hi Raymond, 

Here at Transform Credit, we only offer cosigner loans. Providing a cosigner means we're able to lend to you, even if your credit score is below 500. If your cosigner trusts you then we will too. Your cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor, colleague or even your in-laws!) who:

- Is a US resident
- Is over 23 
- Has a credit score of more than 700
- Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time.
- Trusts you and will pay if you do not 
- Will give correct contact details and sign the agreement

More information about our cosigner requirements can be found here: https://transformcredit.com/faq. Send this link to your cosigner so they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/SABA5C9R

If you are not able to find a cosigner, please let me know by responding to this email with "CANCEL". You can reapply at any time by visiting: https://transformcredit.com

Thanks,
Transform Credit

interactions.body:
(Borrower CreditBuilder - CANCEL Email) [RE:Cancel my account] Hi Raymond,
 
Thanks for getting in touch.
 
I can see that on 10/30/2025 you signed up for a CreditBuilder account AND applied for a $5000 loan. I can confirm that I have canceled your Together Loans CreditBuilder, you will not be charged again.

The Together Loans CreditBuilder helps build your credit, even if you do not borrow from us. 

When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 1-2 years. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as interest against a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the loan term is completed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, Together Loans can help you access funds too! At which point, any CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

You can find out more about the Together Loans CreditBuilder here - https://togetherloans.com/together-loans-credit-builder/

We still have your loan application approved for you. All you need is a cosigner, a friend or a family member who has a 700+ credit score and agrees to make the payment if you can't. 

If you need help, please let me know
 
Have a great day, 
James, Together Loans

Once the loan term is completed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, your CreditBuilder account is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, Together Loans can help you access funds too! At which point, any CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

You can find out more about the Together Loans CreditBuilder here - https://togetherloans.com/together-loans-credit-builder/ . However, I can confirm that I have canceled your CreditBuilder account, you will not be charged again. 

We still have your loan application approved for you. All you need is a cosigner, a friend or a family member who has a 750+ credit score and agrees to make the payment if you can't. Follow this link to pick up where you left off: https://tglns.com/s/O3DXD8L6

If you need help, please let me know
 
Have a great day, 
Jade @ Together Loans CreditBuilder

(AI CB ORANGE - Already cancelled Email) [RE: CreditBuilder Payment Reminder] Hi Raymond,

Thanks for getting in touch.

When you applied for a loan with us, we offered two different products: Loans up to $10,000 with a co-signer, and CreditBuilder which is a small loan held on our customer's behalf that customers make monthly payments against, building up their credit score.

I can see that you signed up for a Together Loans CreditBuilder account when applying for a loan of $5000, on 10/30/2025. We sent you a ‘Welcome to Credit Builder' email on sign-up, confirming your account, how it works and how you can manage it.

Your CreditBuilder helps build your credit, even if you do not borrow from us. When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 1-2 years. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as interest against a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the loan term is completed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

However, I can confirm that your CreditBuilder account has been canceled, so no further payments will be taken. If you wish to set-up a new CreditBuilder account, you can do so here https://tglns.com/s/HKXL7C1B. CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. You can find out more about CreditBuilder here: https://togetherloans.com/together-loans-credit-builder/

Together Loans can still help you access funds of up to $10,000 with the help of a cosigner. You can always access your application here: https://tglns.com/s/O3DXD8L6

If you need help, please let me know.

Many thanks,
Ben, Together Loans

interactions.body:
[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : *****************90301] Hi Kemel,

Thank you for reaching out.

When you applied for a loan with us, we offered two different products: Loans up to $7000 with a co-signer, and CreditBuilder which is a small loan held on our customer's behalf that customers make monthly payments against, building up their credit score.

The CreditBuilder is offered to everyone that applies for a loan with us, and our customers will have the choice to sign up or not sign up for the CreditBuilder loan, as part of the application process. I can see that on August 14th, when you applied for this loan of $3250, you also reached the page where the CreditBuilder product was explained and offered, and you chose to sign up when clicked on “ok, set up my account”. I've attached a screenshot of this page to this email. 

After signing up, we immediately sent you a “Welcome to Transform CreditBuilder!” email, confirming your account, and further explaining the benefits of the product you had opted into. I can see this was sent to you on August 14th to kemel.******@yahoo.com. The email also provided you with your login link, so you could access your account and manage/cancel it, at any time, even before the first payment.

When you signed up for CreditBuilder, you took the first step in building your score. Each month you pay $5, we report these payments to the Credit Bureaus as interest against the loan that has been held on your behalf. These successful payments build your credit history, helping to improve your score. The great thing about CreditBuilder is that if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full and so missed payments can never undo the positive impact that your on-time payments have been making on your credit score. Our customers use CreditBuilder as a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. 

We also sent you a payment reminder before collecting your CreditBuilder payment, to your email, reminding you that your $5 payment was due. This again provided you with your login details, where you were able to manage or cancel your CreditBuilder, should you have decided you no longer wanted to benefit from the product.

Based on the above, we believe we acted in line with your instructions. However, we now understand that you may not have intentionally signed-up to CreditBuilder, and, so as a gesture of goodwill, we have issued you a refund to put you back in the position you were in, before opening your CreditBuilder account. 

We will also close your account, meaning we will not attempt any further payments.

We understand that confusion can sometimes arise and we appreciate your patience as we work to rectify this situation. Please allow 3 business days for the refund to show in your account.  

Many thanks,
Denzel@TransformCredit

[Affordability Confirmation] To Chad ******,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to charges of criminal fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

IF THE INFORMATION BELOW IS CORRECT, YOU DO NOT NEED TO REPLY TO THIS EMAIL.

Information provided to Transform Credit by you:

Borrower: Chad ******
IP: 209.94.187.82
DateTime (UTC): 01-16-2023
Stated Monthly Income: Full Time Employed $3600
Date of next income: 01-20-2023
Rent: $598
Bills: $200
Other Expenses: $350
Your Credit Commitments: $300
Transform Credit Repayment: $177.13
To be safe: $50
Total available after expenditure and this loan repayment: $1924.87


Yours sincerely
Transform credit Applications Dept

Hi Chad, Thanks for reaching out. Transform Credit is a cosigner-based lender, which means all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them. If you cannot find a cosigner, we can cancel your application if you prefer. You can apply again anytime! We can help you as soon as you find a cosigner. You can reply CANCEL, or use this link to continue when you are ready: https://tcrdt.com/s/E5Q0MWYA If you have any other questions, just let us know.
Thanks, Transform Credit

interactions.body: (Requesting lower loan without a GT SMS) Hi Chase, Transform Credit requires a cosigner, regardless of the requested loan amount. A cosigner shows us that someone trusts you and that we should too, regardless of your credit score. A suitable cosigner is someone who has a very good credit history with no trouble paying bills in the past, and who can afford to make the monthly payments if you don't. Although they don't have to be a homeowner, it may increase the possibility of us accepting them as your cosigner if they do own their own home. If you need to update your loan amount or term, you can do so here: https://tcrdt.com/s/IJLJMD2F Thanks, TransformCredit

interactions.body:
(Appeals Auto Response BRW Email) [RE:Co- signer??? To whom it may concern:] Hi Yolanda, 

Thanks for contacting Appeals. 

We understand that it can be disheartening when you're struggling to find a suitable cosigner, don't worry though - the most important thing is we've already accepted your application and want to help you.

There are a few reasons we could find someone to be unsuitable - the most common being a less than perfect credit history. It will be up to your cosigner to make any loan payments that you do not, looking at their Experian credit history allows us to check that they can manage this in addition to their existing commitments. If a potential cosigner has missed or late payments, we can't be certain they'd be able to afford the loan payments while maintaining their other accounts. Alternatively, If your potential cosigner's credit file is locked, we won't have been able to confirm either way (they can apply as soon as their credit file is unlocked: https://tcrdt.com/s/QWMOZA8W). 

When you're considering who to ask, remember your cosigner must have a 750+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time (homeowners and mortgage payers are more likely to be accepted). It's important to remember that your cosigner can only be on one loan with Together Loans at a time too.

If you've provided someone who is unsure why they weren't accepted, we recommend they check their Experian credit report/score for free at: https://www.experian.com/.

In the meantime, the $3000 will still be reserved for you. We'd love for your loan to add to the $100 million we've already paid out to people just like you. Give this link to your cosigner to register online: https://tcrdt.com/s/QWMOZA8W and feel free to check out our FAQs for further help: https://https://togetherloans.com/faq.

Jade@Together Loans

P.S. I am ready to pay $3000 out within 24 hours of your cosigner being approved, but if you don't plan to provide a new cosigner please let me know by replying CANCEL so I can reserve it for someone else

interactions.body:
(AI BW GTQs - Can I get loan without GT Email) [RE: To whom it may concern:] Hi Yolanda,

Based on your situation, we require you to provide a cosigner. 

A cosigner shows us that someone trusts you to repay the loan, and that we should too! This allows us to lend to you even if you've been turned down elsewhere. We offer loans between $3000 and $10,000, depending on where you live and how long you wish to make the repayments for.

If you could get a loan elsewhere at a cheaper rate, without the need for a cosigner, it suggests that Together Loans might not be the right choice for you. You should also go with the best option on the market for you. 

If you do have a suitable cosigner, send them this link and they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/M28PHI7W

Thanks,
Together Loans

(Borrower GT Default Email) [RE:To whom it may concern:] Hi Yolanda, 

Here at Together Loans, we only offer cosigner loans. Providing a cosigner means we're able to lend to you, even if your credit score is below 500. If your cosigner trusts you then we will too. Your cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor, colleague or even your in-laws!) who:

- Is a US resident
- Is over 23 
- Has a credit score of more than 700
- Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time.
- Trusts you and will pay if you do not 
- Will give correct contact details and sign the agreement

More information about our cosigner requirements can be found here: https://togetherloans.com/faq. Send this link to your cosigner so they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/M28PHI7W

If you are not able to find a cosigner, please let me know by responding to this email with CANCEL. You can reapply at any time by visiting: https://togetherloans.com

Thanks,
Together Loans

interactions.body: (AI BW GTQs - GT requirements SMS) Hi Michael, a cosigner is someone who trusts you and agrees to make any repayments you don't. Most of our customers ask a friend or family member. Suitable cosigners have a credit score of 750+ and a history of paying bills on time. Homeowners don't usually need a score as high as 750 and anyone with over a 700 score has a fair chance of being accepted. If you're unsure if your cosigner will be accepted, ask them to complete their application online, and they will be informed if they are accepted. If you need to provide a new cosigner, use this link: https://tcrdt.com/s/IQ9HE9VA Reply CANCEL to unsubscribe. Thanks, Transform Credit

interactions.body: (Borrower GT Rule 1 SMS) Hi Michael, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://togetherloans.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/MGG2BJI3 - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Together Loans

interactions.body:
(Borrower GT Default Email) [RE:Need help finding a cosigner?] Hi Jennifer, 

Here at Transform Credit, we only offer cosigner loans. Providing a cosigner means we're able to lend to you, even if your credit score is below 500. If your cosigner trusts you then we will too. Your cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor, colleague or even your in-laws!) who:

- Is a US resident
- Is over 23 
- Has a credit score of more than 700
- Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time.
- Trusts you and will pay if you do not 
- Will give correct contact details and sign the agreement

More information about our cosigner requirements can be found here: https://transformcredit.com/faq. Send this link to your cosigner so they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/F0JLGJF8

If you are not able to find a cosigner, please let me know by responding to this email with "CANCEL". You can reapply at any time by visiting: https://transformcredit.com

Thanks,
Transform Credit

We have a responsibility to make sure that cosigning your loan is something that they would be able to financially support and based on the information provided we don't feel that this is the case for them. A good cosigner has a credit score of 750+, and a good history of paying their bills on time. Homeowners (not tenants) are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time

[Affordability Confirmation] To Porfilio ******,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to litigation for misrepresentation and fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

IF THE INFORMATION BELOW IS CORRECT, YOU DO NOT NEED TO REPLY TO THIS EMAIL.

Information provided to Transform Credit by you:

Borrower: Porfilio ******
IP: 67.198.73.177
DateTime (UTC): 06-20-2025
Stated Monthly Income: Retired $9700
Rent: $0
Bills: $300
Other Expenses: $500
Your Credit Commitments: $0
Transform Credit Repayment: $237.620000
To be safe: $50

Yours sincerely
Transform credit Applications Dept

[Affordability Confirmation] To Cynthia Apodaca,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to charges of criminal fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

IF THE INFORMATION BELOW IS CORRECT, YOU DO NOT NEED TO REPLY TO THIS EMAIL.

Information provided to Transform Credit by you:

interactions.body:
(Borrower GT Rule 1 Email) [RE:You asked us to help with $3000 - and we can!] Hi Rudolph, 

Thanks for reaching out, we understand that it can seem difficult to find a cosigner at first. Don't worry though, we help people like you every day!

We're a cosigner lender, all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them. A cosigner is a great sign that someone trusts you, and that we should too regardless of your credit history! If your cosigner trusts you, then we will.

A cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor or even a colleague) who is a US resident, has a 750+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time - someone who owns their home or pays a mortgage is much more likely to be accepted. Remember, as long as you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to make a payment! You might find some useful tips on how to approach finding or speaking to a potential cosigner here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans

When you have found someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/WWEFFSQL - we'd love for your loan to add to the $25million we've already paid out to people just like you. If you still find that you're not able to find a cosigner, please respond to this email with CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else.

Thanks, 
Jade@Transform Credit

interactions.body:
(GT - Email - settling loan) [RE:There is a Pending ACH on your Account - 120388CAPS] Hi Kenneth,
 
Your current balance is $4782.04.  
 
To pay off your loan, please follow: https://tcrdt.com/s/CP56WCMY

We will need to receive the funds within 5 working days to honor this figure. After 5 working days, the balance you need to pay will update to include the additional daily accrued interest. This payoff figure also excludes any other changes to the balance that may occur, aside from accrued interest, such as a returned payment.
 
Thanks,
TransformCredit

(AI GT Confirming Payment - Pending payment - GT DD - Arrears Email) [RE: There is a Pending ACH on your Account - 120388CAPS] Hi Kenneth,

There is a pending payment of $195.80, from your bank account. ACH payments can take up to 5 days to clear, at which point the payment will show on your account.

Will this payment be successful? This loan is currently 16 days past due, so if this payment isn't successful it's crucial the account is brought current immediately, to prevent extra interest accruing and avoid further escalation.

You can make a payment immediately here: https://tcrdt.com/s/DU3PCHBU or pay late here: https://tcrdt.com/s/NCWG5TDY

Thanks,
Mia, Transform Credit

(GT - Email - balance and statement of account) [RE:There is a Pending ACH on your Account - 120388CAPS] Hi Kenneth,
 
Your balance as of today is $4782.04. Please bear in mind that we charge our interest daily against the balance, therefore this amount will change on a daily basis.
 
You can access your statement of account any time via this link: https://tcrdt.com/s/906QEZBS
 
If you are looking to settle your loan, please reply  with "settle my loan"
 
Thanks,
TransformCredit

[victoriahall] spk_0: Please leave your message for
spk_1: 619-88.

[Your TransformCredit Loan : 120388CAPS] Hi Kenneth,

To settle your loan, download our App https://tcrdt.com/s/N8MP0EPX or go online: https://tcrdt.com/s/CP56WCMY If the pending payment is going to clear, you can deduct that amount.

Funds must be received within five working days to honour the settlement amount and waive off any interest accumulated during this time frame. Otherwise, you will be charged for the account's interest accumulated during the time frame. 

If you have any questions, feel free to reach out. We're here to assist you.

Thanks,
Transform Credit

(Appeals Auto Response BRW Email) [RE:Application] Hi Brandi, 

Thanks for contacting Appeals. 

We understand that it can be disheartening when you're struggling to find a suitable cosigner, don't worry though - the most important thing is we've already accepted your application and want to help you.

There are a few reasons we could find someone to be unsuitable - the most common being a less than perfect credit history. It will be up to your cosigner to make any loan payments that you do not, looking at their Experian credit history allows us to check that they can manage this in addition to their existing commitments. If a potential cosigner has missed or late payments, we can't be certain they'd be able to afford the loan payments while maintaining their other accounts. Alternatively, If your potential cosigner's credit file is locked, we won't have been able to confirm either way (they can apply as soon as their credit file is unlocked: https://tcrdt.com/s/HD7TLEPG). 

When you're considering who to ask, remember your cosigner must have a 750+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time (homeowners and mortgage payers are more likely to be accepted). It's important to remember that your cosigner can only be on one TransformCredit loan at a time too.

If you've provided someone who is unsure why they weren't accepted, we recommend they check their Experian credit report/score for free at: https://www.experian.com/.

In the meantime, the $3000 will still be reserved for you. We'd love for your loan to add to the $100 million we've already paid out to people just like you. Give this link to your cosigner to register online: https://tcrdt.com/s/HD7TLEPG and feel free to check out our FAQs for further help: https://transformcredit.com/faq.

Jade@TransformCredit 

P.S. I am ready to pay $3000 out within 24 hours of your cosigner being approved, but if you don't plan to provide a new cosigner please let me know by replying CANCEL so I can reserve it for someone else

Message from MessageFactoryBot — INVITE GT BRW SMS DSIT1 PST

interactions.body: Hi Joseorlando. Thank you for reaching out. We understand this situation has been disappointing. While checking, I could confirm that, unfortunately, your previous cosigner has failed our final checks and therefore is not suitable for your loan. Due to data protection, I can't share any specific reason, we have sent them an email explaining the reason behind this decision. Thank you for contacting Transform Credit. However, I can confirm that at this time, our infrastructure only supports being able to lend to those that can understand English - our contracts, website, communications, and staff are not set up to support other languages. Being a borrower or a cosigner is a serious responsibility and it would be unlawful for us to lend to someone where we knew there was an obvious language barrier, putting that person into a vulnerable situation where they may not fully understand the agreement they are entering into.  We appreciate your understanding. We can still pay out your $5000 once you find a suitable cosigner. Remember, a suitable cosigner is someone who has a credit score of more than 700 and a good history of paying their bills on time. Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time. Most of our borrowers ask a close friend or family member to be their cosigner.
